question_text: Which of the following is a longitudinal wave?

options.label: A
options.text: A sound wave travelling through air

options.label: B
options.text: A light wave travelling from the Sun

options.label: C
options.text: A wave on the surface of water

options.label: D
options.text: A wave on a guitar string

explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal because the particles vibrate back and forth in the same direction as the wave travels. Light waves, water waves, and guitar string waves are all transverse because the particles vibrate at right angles to the direction of travel.
